item.title: NLC to Transition to Separate Regional Leagues in 2027
item.content:encoded:
The Northern League of Legends Championship will transition to two separate regional leagues in 2027 in a major restructuring announced by Riot.

Following NLC’s 2026 Spring and Summer splits, the 2027 restructuring will decouple the ‘UK and Ireland’ from the ‘Nordic countries.’

Recounting Leagues ApS collapse

The news follows the ignominious collapse of Leagues ApS, which had informed Riot Games in March that it was “no longer in a position to operate the league.”

The news had followed months of reports and social media accusations of missed payments to broadcast talent, with the Winter split of the NLC stripping the product of the majority of its established broadcast talent.

The result was a Winter Split that drew significant criticism for its lack of professionalism, combined with a diluted competitive product due to the promotion of co-streamers and ‘Wildcard’ substitute players.

Following the news that Leagues ApS could no longer run the NLC, Riot stated that they were “actively assessing the situation and aligning on next steps to ensure continuity and stability for the competition, adding that they would “share more details on what this means for the Spring Split and the path ahead before it begins at the end of March.”

GameWaves and Riot outline the future of the NLC

In reality, March and April came and passed with no update from Riot, but they evidently did eventually find what they regard as a safe custodian of the NLC.

GameWaves will act as the Tournament Organizer for the NLC Spring and Summer Splits. The TO will operate Road of Legends, the Benelux ERL, and will now take the helm of the NLC alongside regional TOs, which will continue to host domestic competitions.

The news may have come late, but Riot and GameWaves have promised to host an albeit truncated NLC Spring 2026 split, featuring the following teams:

  • Verdant
  • Bulldog
  • Arctic Pandas
  • DMG Esports
  • LEO
  • Deer Gaming
  • Lunqvist Lightside
  • Ruddy Corporation

The eight teams will compete in a Best of 1 Round-Robin group stage before a five-team double-elimination playoff bracket, with the winner securing an EMEA Masters Spring Split berth.

The NLC Summer 2026 Split is yet to be outlined, but will likely more closely resemble a normal NLC split.

Following the conclusion of the 2026 competitions, the NLC will split into two regional leagues, with the Nordic region and the ‘UK and Ireland’ region becoming distinct regions.

The move separates the two regions for the first time since 2019, when the UK League Championship (UKLC) was consolidated with national leagues in Finland, Norway, Denmark, Iceland, and Sweden to form the NLC.

As part of the announcement, Riot explained the separation, stating, “With the continued development of the ERL ecosystem, we have seen the increasing benefits of the decentralised model and believe this is the right moment to further the transition and split the region into two competitive tracks: UK & Ireland and the Nordic countries.

“We’re excited about what this unlocks: more opportunities for players through local competitions, the chance to crown National Champions, and live events closer to home for fans across the region.”

Do you think this is the right move for UK, Irish, and Nordic League? Let us know on our Discord!
For more news, see Team Heretics Reunite Nukeduck with Mithy as LEC Head Coach.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.title: Sega’s Super Game Has Been Canceled
item.content:encoded:
According to the latest financial results, Sega has decided to cancel its Super Game initiative and provided a list of games in its pipeline.

In a presentation held in 2021, Sega announced plans to reboot several dormant titles while also working on a new ‘Super Game’ project. Since that announcement, we’ve learned that the company is launching the Sega Universe to revive classic franchises. However, there has been no new information regarding the Super Game until now.

Sega Super Game No Longer in Development

In the latest financial results, Sega announced it had decided to cancel the Super Game and informed investors that there were no additional costs associated with the cancellation. The decision comes after the company realized that free-to-play games have struggled in FY2026. Sonic Rumble Party’s performance was weak; it failed to generate economic value through its collaboration with Rovio. Additionally, some game launches were delayed.

After delaying the Super Game, Sega has revealed the next titles they have in the works. The closest release is Stranger Than Heaven, which is set to arrive this Winter. Post that, the developer has games like Persona 4 Revival, Total War: Warhammer 40,000, Crazy Taxi, Streets of Rage, and more; however, none of their release dates/windows have been revealed. Check out the entire list of games in Sega’s pipeline below:

  • Stranger Than Heaven (This Winter)
  • Persona 4 Revival (Release Date TBD)
  • Total War: WARHAMMER 40,000 (Release Date TBD)
  • Total War: Medieval III (Release Date TBD)
  • New VIRTUA FIGHTER Project (Title/Release Date TBD)
  • Crazy Taxi (Title/Release Date TBD)
  • Golden Axe (Title/Release Date TBD)
  • Jet Set Radio (Title/Release Date TBD)
  • Streets of Rage (Title/Release Date TBD)
  • ALIEN: ISOLATION (Title/Release Date TBD)

In other news, LEGO has revealed the SEGA Genesis (Mega Drive) Console set, coming in just a few weeks for a steal of a price. Additionally, Sega has addressed game development costs and will strengthen data analysis to optimize digital sales in the immediate future. What are your thoughts on Sega canceling the Super Game? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Lies of P developer hiring a generative AI creator. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Who Is Devil Dinosaur? Marvel Rivals Season 8 Character, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Season 8 of Marvel Rivals is almost here, and as has become custom, that means two new characters are expected to join the roster over the next two months or so. Everything is pointing to two characters in particular, Cyclops and Devil Dinosaur, with the latter likely to be the first addition, thanks to an early teaser.

However, just like some characters in prior seasons of Marvel Rivals, plenty of you might be unfamiliar with Devil Dinosaur; after all, they’re a relatively niche character in the Marvel universe, especially compared to this season’s accomplice, Cyclops.

That’s why we’re here to spill the beans on everything Devil Dinosaur. From their first comic appearance to their character traits and history, by the end of the article, you should be more than ready for the lovable Dino to step onto the Marvel Rivals stage.

Who Is Devil Dinosaur?

Devil Dinosaur was first introduced as a giant red Tyrannosaurus Rex who lived in Dinosaur World, a version of Earth in a parallel universe where dinosaurs co-exist with primitive humanoid tribes.

He got his fiery red appearance after he was nearly burned to death by a tribe of Killer-Folk, but Moon-Boy, a young member of a rival tribe, rescued him. Not only did the fire change his skin, but it also activated dormant mutations inside him, giving him greater powers than the rest of his species.

  • More Insider Gaming: Marvel Rivals Season 8 Start Date & New Heroes

Devil Dinosaur proceeded to team up with Moon-Boy, as the two formed an unbreakable bond. They embarked on numerous sporadic adventures throughout the Marvel universe, including teaming up with Godzilla and relocating to the Savage Land in the main Earth-616 continuity.

However, Moon-Boy eventually meets his end, and Moon-Girl takes over as Devil Dinosaur’s new companion. Thanks to her Inhuman powers, she can switch minds with Devil Dinosaur, putting her in control of his body while the dinosaur inhabits her human form.

The two are inexplicably tied together and crucial to one another’s story. Moon Girl’s impact on Devil Dinosaur in Marvel Rivals remains to be seen, but don’t be surprised if his character has some mind-switching abilities, perhaps tied to his Ultimate.

What Are Devil Dinosaur’s Abilities?

Devil Dinosaur has a notable list of abilities, though the obvious one is the fact that they’re a giant T-Rex!

In the comics, Devil Dinosaur is generally presented as having a surprisingly high intelligence, especially for a dinosaur. It’s arguable that he’s as smart, or potentially smarter, than the average human, though he typically can’t speak.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Vote During Marvel Rivals Hellfire Gala 2026

As for his power, he’s naturally strong as a dinosaur, but his strength exceeds the typical threshold for a T-Rex. To add to that, his mutant powers make him extremely resistant to heat and practically bulletproof.

He’s even prone to fluctuating in size, though it’s unclear whether that’s an inherent ability or an unintended side effect of his mutation—or a quick explanation for comic continuity!

How these abilities present themself in Marvel Rivals remains to be seen, but you shouldn’t have to wait too long, as he’s bound to be officially revealed over the coming days, so keep an eye out!

Do you feel like you have a better understanding of Devil Dinosaur now? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Marvel Rivals content, check out the following:

  • How To Claim All Marvel Rivals Path To Doomsday Rewards: The Avengers Event
  • How To Play Path To Doomsday: The Avengers Game Mode In Marvel Rivals—Loki Guide

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
